signals.headline: Confidence: LOW** – Search results lack any "just confirmed" events (e
signals.raw: Confidence: LOW** – Search results lack any "just confirmed" events (e.g., upgrades, ETF approvals, or resolutions) post-April 14; focus remains on ongoing technicals and prior data without market-moving developments today.[1][2][3][4][5][6]

signals.headline: **South Korea Annual Inflation Update**: March 2026 CPI confirmed at 2
signals.raw: **South Korea Annual Inflation Update**: March 2026 CPI confirmed at 2.2% YoY (from 2.0% Feb, vs 2.4% forecast), core at 2.2%; Polymarket odds cluster mid-2% bins (24-28%) for full-year 2026, ahead of April data and May BoK meeting; IMF/AMRO forecast 2.3-2.5% amid won weakness[1][6].
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: **Prediction Market Impact**: Warsh nomination + confirmed CPI/inflation data shift Fed hike odds and SK inflation bins,
signals.raw: **Prediction Market Impact**: Warsh nomination + confirmed CPI/inflation data shift Fed hike odds and SK inflation bins, moving rate cut probabilities near zero and tightening SK mid-2% contestation[1][2].

signals.headline: **Fed Chair Transition**: Kevin Warsh, former Fed Governor, nominated to succeed Jerome Powell, potentially ushering in
signals.raw: **Fed Chair Transition**: Kevin Warsh, former Fed Governor, nominated to succeed Jerome Powell, potentially ushering in a more inflation-focused policy; markets now price ~20% chance of rate hike by Dec 2026 per JPMorgan, with Fed holding rates steady and dot plot showing split (7 expect no cuts, 5 expect two)[2][3][5].
matched_markets: Kevin Warsh confirmed as Fed Chair by May 15?

signals.headline: **US Inflation Surge**: Annual core rate hit 3
signals.raw: **US Inflation Surge**: Annual core rate hit 3.9% (pre-oil shock), doubling Fed's 2% target on PCE basis; energy costs and oil shocks amplify pressures, with forecasts at 3.2-4% by end-2026[2][3][4][6].
